name: Chicken Roti
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 pounds Chicken, skinned, boned and cut into 1-1/2 inch cubes
1/2 teaspoon Scotch Bonnet pepper, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. To prepare the pooris, combine the flour, baking powder, and salt in a mixing bowl. Pour the oil into the water, then add this mixture to the flour mixture to form a dough. Mix well. Knead for about 5 minutes until the dough is very smooth. Divide into 6 balls of dough. Set aside covered with a damp cloth for about 2 hours.
2. Using a rolling pin, roll each ball into a thin flattened circle, a poori, about 9 inches in diameter. Set aside covered.
3. To prepare the filling, combine the chicken and all other ingredients except the oil and garnish. Mix well and marinate for 2 hours or longer.
4. When done marinating, add 2 tablespoons oil to a saucepan and heat to medium. Add the chicken mixture and cook, stirring constantly for about 5 minutes. Add water then cook on low for about 5 minutes, or until chicken is tender. Keep warm.
5. Add 2 tablespoons of oil to a skillet or grill. Place the rolled pooris on the skillet. Fry each side for about 2 minutes, or until lightly browned. Brush each side with margarine or oil. Wrap in clean towel to keep warm until ready to serve.
6. When ready to serve, fold about 2 tablespoons of chicken mixture into each round and serve. Garnish with scallions and Scotch Bonnet pepper.
